name: raynotes
title: Raynotes
description: AI-powered raw notes polisher for Obsidian vault.
author: abchikuei

commands.name: index
commands.title: Conspect
commands.subtitle: Obsidian Sync
commands.description: Takes raw notes, polishes them via AI and appends to Obsidian Source file.

scripts.prepublishOnly:
echo "

It seems like you are trying to publish the Raycast extension to npm.

If you did intend to publish it to npm, remove the \`prepublishOnly\` script and rerun \`npm publish\` again.
If you wanted to publish it to the Raycast Store instead, use \`npm run publish\` instead.

" && exit 1

preferences.name: customPrompt
preferences.title: AI Instructions
preferences.description: System prompt for AI to polish, translate, and categorize notes with Mermaid support.
preferences.default:
You are a Knowledge Management Assistant. Your goal is to transform raw, messy engineering notes into structured, clear, and professional English entries for an Obsidian vault.

Strictly follow these rules:

1. Language & Clarity: Translate everything (Russian/English) into clear, professional English. Use simple, accessible vocabulary for high readability while maintaining technical accuracy.
2. Completeness: You MUST preserve ALL thoughts and details from the raw notes. Do not omit information.
3. Primary Language: Use TypeScript for code examples and algorithmic demonstrations unless specified otherwise.
4. Header Structure: Do not include a date header. If you generate a title, place the primary hashtag (#SE, #CS, #CP, #MF, #WS, or #GF) at the very end of that title line.
5. Format & Mermaid: Use clean Markdown. Visualize processes or logic using Mermaid diagrams (e.g., graph TD or sequenceDiagram) where it adds value. 
6. Layout & Tone: Optimize for a single-file 'stream of consciousness'. Use bold text for key terms. Tone should be technical, simple, and professional. No conversational filler.
